title: Waste Management Decision Flowchart
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive decision tree using vis-network where students navigate waste management choices, comparing environmental impact scores for each pathway

educational.topic: Waste Management and the Waste Hierarchy
learningObjectives: Prioritize waste management strategies using the waste hierarchy and evaluate trade-offs
Analyze the environmental consequences of different waste disposal methods
Apply the waste hierarchy to real-world waste decisions
prerequisites: Basic understanding of solid waste and pollution

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive decision-making with environmental impact scoring
keyQuestions: Why is waste reduction more effective than recycling from an environmental standpoint?
What are the hidden environmental costs of landfilling versus composting?
How do decomposition times vary across different materials in landfills?
When might incineration be a better option than landfilling?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think recycling is the best environmental option, not realizing that reduction and reuse are significantly better
Students may believe that all materials decompose within a few years in landfills, not understanding that many plastics persist for centuries
Students sometimes think composting and landfilling are similar since both involve burying waste
assessmentOpportunities: Have students trace flowchart paths for common household waste items and compare impact scores
Ask students to conduct a school waste audit and propose hierarchy-based improvements
Students design a community waste management plan prioritizing top-of-hierarchy strategies
